depression.description: I can't shake off the sadness I feel since my colleague left. It feels like everything is falling apart at work, and I'm left feeling lonely and unsupported.
I often think that I'm unlovable and that no one truly wants me around. This belief makes it hard for me to connect with others, even when I crave that connection.
As the lab meeting approaches, I feel an intense pressure to be perfectly prepared. If things don't go well, I worry it will reflect poorly on me.

depression.topic: Fear of losing control at work
depression.description: I feel like I'm losing control over my work environment, especially after my colleague left. It's as if the stability I relied on is crumbling, and I don't know how to cope.

Delusion.topic: Belief that I must always be productive
Delusion.description: I feel like I need to constantly be busy and engaged to prove my worth. If I'm not productive, I fear I won't be valued or loved.

Delusion.topic: Worry about the future of the lab
Delusion.description: With my colleague gone, I can't help but think that the lab will struggle without them. I feel responsible for holding everything together, but I don't know if I can.
